content.text:
Here's a clear picture of the current state and what's needed for mobile compatibility testing:

## Current E2E Test Setup

The existing e2e tests (`tests/e2e/`) use plain `fetch()` calls — they check HTTP status and HTML content but have **no browser automation, no viewport testing, and no visual rendering**. They can't detect mobile layout issues.

## Recommended Approach: Playwright

The best fit for mobile compatibility testing here is **Playwright**, which supports:
- Built-in device emulation (exact viewport + user-agent for real devices)
- Visual screenshot comparison
- Touch event simulation
- No real device required

Playwright is already present in `node_modules` (via Next.js's `experimental/testmode`), but not set up as a test runner.

## Proposed Coverage

**Devices to emulate** (Playwright has built-in profiles for most of these):

| Brand | Models | Width |
|---|---|---|
| Apple | iPhone SE | 375px |
| Apple | iPhone 14 Pro / 15 Pro | 393px |
| Apple | iPhone 14 Pro Max / 15 Plus | 430px |
| Apple | iPad Mini | 744px |
| Apple | iPad Pro 12.9" | 1024px |
| Google | Pixel 5 / 7 | 393px |
| Samsung | Galaxy S21 / S23 | 360px |
| Generic Android | 360–412px range | — |

**What to test on each:**

1. **Layout integrity** — no horizontal overflow (`document.body.scrollWidth <= window.innerWidth`)
2. **Navigation** — hamburger/mobile menu renders and is tappable
3. **Font sizes** — body text >= 16px (prevents iOS auto-zoom on inputs)
4. **Touch targets** — buttons/links >= 44×44px (Apple HIG minimum)
5. **Images** — scale within container, no overflow
6. **Code blocks / tables** — horizontal scroll within container, not full page
7. **Search modal** — opens correctly, keyboard doesn't push layout
8. **TOC** — hidden/collapsed on mobile, accessible
9. **Series sidebar** — collapsed or converted to dropdown on small screens
10. **Reading progress bar** — renders correctly

## Implementation Plan

1. Install `@playwright/test` + browser binaries
2. Add `playwright.config.ts` with device matrix
3. Create `tests/e2e/mobile.spec.ts` with the above checks
4. Add `test:mobile` script to `package.json`

---

Want me to go ahead and implement this? I can start with a focused set of devices (e.g., iPhone SE, iPhone Pro Max, Pixel 5, Galaxy S21, iPad) and the most critical checks (overflow, navigation, touch targets), which gives broad coverage without excessive test runtime.

{
  "name": "@hutusi/amytis",
  "version": "1.11.0",
  "description": "A high-performance digital garden and blog engine with Next.js 16 and Tailwind CSS v4",
  "repository": {
    "type": "git",

    "new-note": "bun scripts/new-note.ts",
    "import-book": "bun scripts/import-book.ts",
    "sync-book": "bun scripts/sync-book-chapters.ts",
    "series-draft": "bun scripts/series-draft.ts",
    "deploy": "bun scripts/deploy.ts",
    "start": "next start",
    "lint": "eslint",
    "test": "bun test src tests/unit tests/tooling packages/create-amytis/src && bun run test:int",
    "test:unit": "bun test src tests/unit packages/create-amytis/src",
    "test:int": "bun test tests/integration",
    "test:e2e": "bun test tests/e2e"
  },
  "dependencies": {
    "@giscus/react": "^3.1.0",
    "@tailwindcss/typography": "^0.5.19",
    "d3": "^7.9.0",
    "github-slugger": "^2.0.0",
    "gray-matter": "^4.0.3",
    "image-size": "^2.0.2",
    "katex": "^0.16.33",
    "mermaid": "^11.12.3",
    "next": "16.1.6",
    "next-image-export-optimizer": "^1.20.1",
    "next-themes": "^0.4.6",
    "react": "19.2.4",
    "react-dom": "19.2.4",
    "react-icons": "^5.5.0",
    "react-markdown": "^10.1.0",
    "react-syntax-highlighter": "^16.1.0",
    "rehype-katex": "^7.0.1",
    "rehype-raw": "^7.0.0",
    "rehype-slug": "^6.0.0",
    "rehype-stringify": "^10.0.1",
    "remark-gfm": "^4.0.1",
    "remark-math": "^6.0.0",
    "remark-parse": "^11.0.0",
    "remark-rehype": "^11.1.2",
    "unified": "^11.0.5",
    "unist-util-visit": "^5.1.0",
    "zod": "^4.3.6"
  },
  "devDependencies": {
    "@playwright/test": "^1.58.2",
    "@tailwindcss/postcss": "^4.1.18",
    "@types/bun": "^1.3.9",
    "@types/d3": "^7.4.3",
    "@types/hast": "^3.0.4",
    "@types/image-size": "^0.8.0",
    "@types/mdast": "^4.0.4",
    "@types/node": "^24.10.13",
    "@types/react": "^19.2.14",
    "@types/react-dom": "^19.2.3",
    "@types/react-syntax-highlighter": "^15.5.13",
    "babel-plugin-react-compiler": "1.0.0",
    "eslint": "^9.0.0",
    "eslint-config-next": "16.1.6",
    "pagefind": "^1.4.0",
    "pdf-to-img": "^5.0.0",
    "tailwindcss": "^4.1.18",
    "typescript": "^5.9.3"
  },
  "ignoreScripts": [
    "sharp",
    "unrs-resolver"
  ],
  "trustedDependencies": [
    "sharp",
    "unrs-resolver"
  ],
  "workspaces": ["packages/*"]
}

### Device coverage

**Apple (WebKit):** iPhone SE · iPhone 14 Pro · iPhone 14 Pro Max · iPad Mini · iPad Pro 11

### Tests per device

1. **Viewport meta** — `width=device-width, initial-scale=1` present
2. **No horizontal overflow** — 6 pages checked (`/`, `/posts`, `/series`, `/tags`, `/archive`, post page)
3. **Navigation variant** — hamburger on phones (<768px), desktop nav on tablets (≥768px)
4. **Mobile menu open/close** — tap to open, × to close
5. **Menu closes on link tap** — navigates and dismisses
6. **Touch targets** — hamburger ≥ 40×40px, theme toggle ≥ 32px
7. **Post sidebar hidden** on viewports < 1024px
8. **HorizontalScroll arrows hidden** on phones
9. **Body font size** ≥ 14px
10. **Images don't overflow** their containers (homepage + post page)
11. **Scroll lock** — `body.style.overflow=hidden` when menu open, cleared on close
12. **Tags page overflow** check
13. **Archive page overflow** check

### Usage

```bash
# Requires dev server running (auto-starts if not)
bun run test:mobile

# Run just one device
bunx playwright test --project="iPhone SE"

# Run with headed browser (visual)
bunx playwright test --headed --project="iPhone 14 Pro"

# Open HTML report after run
bunx playwright show-report
```
